stories.title: Honest coherence must be earned, not copied
stories.need: know whether coherence scores are earned rather than copied
stories.value: prevent false governance narratives from passing release

actors.name: Studio Operator
actors.responsibility: requests or reviews delivery readiness and receives release guidance
actors.value_received: confidence that release decisions are backed by evidence instead of narrative drift

actors.name: Delivery Taxonomy Owner
actors.responsibility: defines canonical delivery value chain data before implementation exists
actors.value_received: stable taxonomy that can explain why each planned file exists

actors.name: Delivery Contract Consumer
actors.responsibility: validates manifests against taxonomy-defined shape and gate semantics
actors.value_received: manifest checks that do not invent policy in code

actors.name: Delivery Orchestrator
actors.responsibility: combines taxonomy, evidence, story checks, and readiness decisions
actors.value_received: deterministic readiness outcomes from declared evidence

actors.name: Delivery Report Renderer
actors.responsibility: renders read-only delivery readiness and taxonomy projections
actors.value_received: human-readable evidence without mutating source truth

actors.name: Delivery CLI Operator
actors.responsibility: invokes validate and check commands without adding policy
actors.value_received: thin operator entrypoint for governed delivery checks

actors.name: Delivery Test Author
actors.responsibility: codifies contract behavior and exploit protection in deterministic tests
actors.value_received: regression coverage for fake evidence and missing file exploits

actors.name: Documentation Projection Agent
actors.responsibility: projects the taxonomy into artifacts other agents can consume without chat context
actors.value_received: a packet and markdown summary that explain the work independently

acceptance_scenarios.name: copied method responsibility lowers coherence
acceptance_scenarios.responsibility: prove copied method responsibility does not earn honest coherence

acceptance_scenarios.name: distinct method responsibility raises coherence
acceptance_scenarios.responsibility: prove distinct responsibilities are treated as coherent when anchors and evidence align

acceptance_scenarios.name: fake evidence or missing files cannot earn release_ready
acceptance_scenarios.responsibility: prove missing evidence and nonexistent changed files are blocked

evidence_artifact_types.description: Terminal output from an executed command that proves a scenario ran.
A file-based evidence artifact whose path can be resolved and inspected.
A proof that a declared file path exists and is a readable file.
The latest codebase story review used to decide local taxonomy, filesystem story, residue, and file economy states.
The latest taxonomy scan used to explain file anchors and local tie-out.
A deterministic delivery readiness report generated from manifest and evidence data.
A governed waiver artifact with authority, reason, expiration, and follow-up.
A persisted delivery lesson record that captures drift, gates, and future regressions.

decision_states.release_ready: all required gates passed and any waivers are valid, explicit, and time-bound
decision_states.release_blocked: at least one required gate failed, evidence is missing, or a waiver is invalid or expired
decision_states.review_required: evidence is insufficient for automation, so a human decision is required before release

rules: A waiver does not erase evidence requirements; it only permits a governed exception for a named gate.
A waiver must be time-bound; an expired waiver is invalid and cannot produce release_ready.
A waiver must name authority; unnamed authority is not a waiver.
A waiver must name follow-up work; otherwise the risk is unbounded.
A waived gate remains visible in the report and cannot silently disappear from the blocking discussion.

release_behavior.required: failure to pass evidence blocks release
release_behavior.review_required: human decision required; no automated release
release_behavior.waived: release may proceed only when a valid, unexpired waiver is present and all other required gates pass

capture_rules: Persist a learning record whenever a delivery is blocked or a repair is performed.
Name at least one regression candidate when a gate catches a real defect.
Record the evidence types used to make the decision.
Record any waiver that influenced the decision.

status_semantics.pass: learning record exists and is populated enough to explain the outcome
status_semantics.review_required: learning record is missing or incomplete and therefore blocks automation

delivery_boundaries.responsibility: define the canonical delivery value chain and gate semantics before implementation exists
define the canonical delivery manifest shape and allowed gate states derived from taxonomy
load delivery manifest artifacts deterministically without mutating source truth
validate delivery manifest instances against canonical LOC delivery taxonomy rules
combine manifest, evidence, and story gate data into deterministic readiness decisions
render read-only delivery readiness reports from computed readiness data
expose validate and check commands without introducing new policy or mutation
protect the manifest contract and reject fake links or missing story fields
prove fake evidence and nonexistent files cannot earn release_ready
represent the honest coherence delivery chain as a canonical positive fixture
model a manifest missing story identity and prove it fails validation
model a manifest missing executable acceptance and prove it fails validation
build deterministic read-only delivery taxonomy projections from the canonical chain without wall-clock input
generate or verify the deterministic delivery taxonomy projection without adding policy
prove the delivery taxonomy projection is deterministic, read-only, and explains every delivery file
score delivery boundaries for lean value deterministically and derive disposition and retirement candidate views
generate the deterministic delivery lean-value ledger without acting on dispositions
prove lean-value scoring is deterministic and never marks a file safe to remove without retirement evidence
validate retirement candidates against active references and retirement evidence before any move or delete
run deterministic retirement preflight checks without moving or deleting candidate files
prove retirement preflight blocks active references and only passes with complete retirement evidence
extract deterministic package taxonomy, Python anchor, and worker-input census reports from the neighboring ai-engine packages
generate or check deterministic package taxonomy census reports for the neighboring ai-engine packages
verify package taxonomy census extraction is deterministic, BOM-safe, anchor-aware, and drift-checkable
publish a read-only taxonomy projection for downstream agents and reviewers
provide a chat-free work packet derived from canonical taxonomy data
provide a human-readable summary of the canonical delivery taxonomy
defer worker packet generation for this read-only taxonomy pass until implementation boundaries are resumed

worker_packet_policy.reason: This pass is read-only taxonomy projection only, so worker-bee packet drafts are deferred until implementation resumes.
criteria_to_generate: planned files still map cleanly to boundaries
agent requests packetization for file groups

lean_value_governance.doctrine: Lean is fewer unjustified files, removed with evidence and without losing value.
confidence_rules: Every value score must include value_score_confidence and confidence_reason.
Low-confidence scores route to review and must not trigger demotion or removal automatically.
Unknown-confidence scores require more evidence before disposition escalation.
visibility_rules: Separate low_value from low_visibility before recommending demotion or removal.
External CLI users, package exports, dynamic imports, generated call surfaces, and docs-only workflows must be treated as possible value until disproven.
